qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded HTTP request body reading without size limit enforcement enables denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (readRequestBody in src/transports/http/server.ts:224-239), same root cause (unbounded body += chunk.toString() with no size check, maxMessageSize defined but never enforced), same impact (DoS via memory exhaustion), same CWE-770.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: API key accepted via query string parameters enables credential leakage in logs and Referer headers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (credential exposure via query string logging vs DoS via unbounded memory allocation). Different root cause (APIKeyAuthProvider query string fallback vs readRequestBody missing size check). Different code locations (apikey.ts vs server.ts).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing audience and issuer validation in OAuth introspection path enables cross-service token acceptance
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (authentication bypass via missing audience validation vs DoS via unbounded memory allocation). Different root cause (IntrospectionValidator not checking aud/iss values vs readRequestBody missing size check). Different code locations (oauth.ts, introspection-validator.ts vs server.ts).

selected_finding.title: Unbounded HTTP request body reading without size limit enforcement enables denial of service

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The http-unbounded-body-dos finding identifies the exact advisory-described vulnerable code: the readRequestBody() method in src/transports/http/server.ts (lines 224-239) accumulates HTTP request body chunks via body += chunk.toString() with no size limit, while maxMessageSize is defined in types.ts but never enforced. The executed PoC confirmed that a 50MB POST body was fully buffered in memory (RSS grew by 181,392 KB) without any size-limit rejection (response was 406, not 413), proving the advisory-described vulnerability.
evidence: Same vulnerable function: readRequestBody() in src/transports/http/server.ts:224-239
Same root cause: body += chunk.toString() with no size tracking, maxMessageSize defined but never referenced
PoC execution: 50MB POST body sent to MCP HTTP server, RSS grew by 181,392 KB, response was 406 (not 413)

poc_executions.observed_effect: MCP HTTP server (v0.2.21) started on port 18080. A 50MB POST body was sent to /mcp. Server RSS grew from 67,936 KB to 249,328 KB (delta: 181,392 KB), confirming the entire 50MB body was buffered in memory without size limit enforcement. Response status was 406 (Not Acceptable, due to missing Accept header), not 413 (Payload Too Large), proving maxMessageSize was not enforced.
